If {{\dfrac{x+1}{(x-3)(x-5)} }} {{\equiv}} {{\dfrac{M}{x-3} }} + {{\dfrac{N}{x-5} }}, then
The solution set of the inequality {{|2x-1|>3}} is
A partial order relation is that which is
solution:
A relation {{R}} on a set {{S}} is a partial order if it simultaneously satisfies three properties for all {{a, b, c \in S}}:

1. Reflexive: {{aRa}} for all {{a \in S}}.
2. Antisymmetric: If {{aRb}} and {{bRa}}, then {{a = b}}.
3. Transitive: If {{aRb}} and {{bRc}}, then {{aRc}}.

Quick Tip: Distinguish from an equivalence relation, which is reflexive, symmetric (not antisymmetric), and transitive. A total order adds comparability: for any {{a,b}}, either {{aRb}} or {{bRa}}.

question: The number of distinct arrangements of the letters of the word ABRACADABRAR is
solution:
The number of distinct arrangements of {{n}} objects where letters repeat with frequencies {{n_1, n_2, \ldots}} is given by the multinomial formula {{\frac{n!}{n_1!\, n_2!\, \cdots}}}.

Count Letter Frequencies:
A-B-R-A-C-A-D-A-B-R-A-R gives {{n = 12}} letters with:
{{\text{A}: 5, \quad \text{B}: 2, \quad \text{R}: 3, \quad \text{C}: 1, \quad \text{D}: 1}}

Apply the Formula:
{{\frac{12!}{5!\cdot 2!\cdot 3!\cdot 1!\cdot 1!} = \frac{479001600}{120 \times 2 \times 6 \times 1 \times 1} = 332640}}

Quick Tip: Always recount letters carefully before computing — one miscount changes everything.

question: A function {{f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}}} is given by {{f(x)=ax+b}}. If {{f(3)=3}} and {{f(4)=5}}, then the values of the constants {{a}} and {{b}} are such that
solution:
Substituting the two given values into {{f(x) = ax+b}} produces a system of two simultaneous linear equations in {{a}} and {{b}}.

Form and Solve the System:
{{3a + b = 3 \quad \cdots (i) \qquad 4a + b = 5 \quad \cdots (ii)}}

Subtracting (i) from (ii): {{a = 2}}. Substituting into (i): {{b = 3 - 6 = -3}}.

Quick Tip: The value of {{a}} is simply the gradient: {{a = \dfrac{f(4)-f(3)}{4-3} = \dfrac{5-3}{1} = 2}}.
